tasks.title: Create bin/status.js with pure helpers and buildStatus()
tasks.description: New file bin/status.js mirroring bin/stop.js structure. Import { readPidFile, isPidAlive } from ./stop.js (DRY). Implement pure helpers: resolvePort(flags, env) => (--port ▸ PORT ▸ 3000) as an integer; buildStatus({running, pid, port, version, activeRuns, dataDir}) => plain status object with fields { running, pid, port, version, activeRuns, dataDir, sqlitePath, api } where sqlitePath = path.join(dataDir,'prism.db') and api = 'reachable' when activeRuns is a number else 'unreachable'. No I/O in helpers. Re-export helpers for tests.
acceptance_criteria: bin/status.js exists and exports { run, readPidFile, isPidAlive, resolvePort, buildStatus, fetchActiveRunCount, formatText, formatJson }
resolvePort returns flags.port when set, else parseInt(env.PORT) when set, else 3000; always an integer
buildStatus is pure (no fs/http/process access) and sets sqlitePath to <dataDir>/prism.db and api based on activeRuns
readPidFile / isPidAlive are imported from ./stop.js, not re-implemented

tasks.title: Implement fetchActiveRunCount() with time-boxed HTTP + client-side count
tasks.description: Add async fetchActiveRunCount(port, deps={}) to bin/status.js. Uses globalThis.fetch (injectable via deps._fetch) to GET http://127.0.0.1:<port>/api/v1/runs?status=running with an AbortController 1500ms timeout. On 200 + JSON array, return { count: runs.filter(r => r && r.status === 'running').length }. On any failure (non-200, network error, timeout, non-array body), return { count: null, reason }. Never throws.
acceptance_criteria: Sends GET to 127.0.0.1:<port>/api/v1/runs?status=running with a ~1.5s abort budget
Counts running client-side (status === 'running'); ignores non-running entries
Returns { count: null } (never throws) on connection refused, timeout, non-200, or non-array body
fetch is injectable via deps._fetch for tests (no real socket needed)

tasks.title: Implement run(flags, deps), formatters, and exit-code contract
tasks.description: Add run(flags={}, deps={}) orchestrator + formatText(status) + formatJson(status) to bin/status.js, following bin/stop.js DI conventions. DI seam: _readPidFile, _isPidAlive, _fetchActiveRunCount, _exit, _stdout, _stderr. Flow: resolve dataDir (flags.dataDir else resolveDataDir({env,packageRoot,homedir})); pid = _readPidFile(dataDir); if pid===null or !_isPidAlive(pid) => build stopped status, print (text or JSON via flags.json), _exit(1). Else port=resolvePort(flags,env); {count}=await _fetchActiveRunCount(port); build running status, print, _exit(0). formatJson = JSON.stringify(status)+'\n'. formatText = aligned key/value block guarded by TTY/NO_COLOR like doctor.js; running-but-unreachable shows 'Active runs  unknown (API not responding on port <n>)'. Wrap direct invocation in a fatal guard writing to stderr + exit 1.
acceptance_criteria: Exit 0 when PID alive, exit 1 when no/stale PID (matches acceptance criteria)
No pid file OR pid not alive => graceful 'prism is not running' text / {running:false,...} JSON, no stack trace
PID alive + API reachable => text summary with pid/port/version/active-runs/dataDir/sqlite; JSON with activeRuns as integer and api:'reachable'
PID alive + API unreachable => running (exit 0), activeRuns null, api 'unreachable', text shows 'unknown'
All external effects injectable via deps (_readPidFile,_isPidAlive,_fetchActiveRunCount,_exit,_stdout,_stderr)

tasks.title: Wire `status` into bin/cli.js (parser, switch, USAGE)
tasks.description: Register the subcommand in bin/cli.js: add runStatus(flags) that requires ./status.js and calls run(flags).catch(err => { stderr.write; exit 1 }); add `case 'status': runStatus(flags); break;` to the switch; add the `prism status [--json] [--port <n>] [--data-dir <path>]` line to USAGE plus a short 'prism status:' options block. --json/--port/--data-dir are already parsed by parseArgv — no parser change needed beyond confirming.
acceptance_criteria: `prism status` dispatches to bin/status.js run()
USAGE text lists `prism status` with its flags and a description block
`prism status --json` and `prism status --port N --data-dir P` route flags correctly
Unknown-subcommand and --help behaviour unchanged

tasks.title: Unit tests: tests/cli.status.test.js (mock PID file + mock HTTP)
tasks.description: New node:test suite mirroring tests/cli.stop.test.js. Use injected deps: fake _readPidFile/_isPidAlive (no real fs/process) and fake _fetchActiveRunCount (no real socket). Capture _stdout/_stderr writes and the _exit code. Cover: (1) no pid file -> stopped, exit 1, text + {running:false}; (2) stale pid (alive=false) -> stopped, exit 1; (3) alive + count=N -> running, exit 0, activeRuns=N, api reachable; (4) alive + count=null -> running, exit 0, activeRuns null, api unreachable, text 'unknown'; (5) --json key/shape for running & stopped; (6) resolvePort precedence flag>env>default; (7) buildStatus pure table incl sqlitePath; (8) malformed pid -> stopped. Run with `npm test` (foreground, never background).
acceptance_criteria: tests/cli.status.test.js passes under `npm test` (foreground)
PID file and HTTP are both mocked via DI — no real server or socket touched
Covers stopped / running-reachable / running-unreachable / --json / resolvePort / buildStatus / malformed-pid cases
Statement coverage of bin/status.js > 90%
Asserts exit codes: 0 for running, 1 for stopped
